untranslatable_reason: Signal requires counting non-consecutive days matching a condition (close[i] < close[i-2]) until reaching a threshold, plus tracking setup-stage highs and detecting multi-day patterns (sell setup = [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] consecutive closes above corresponding closes [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] days earlier). The backtest engine structure does not support: (1) stateful countdown accumulation across non-consecutive bars, (2) historical lookback windowing with conditional accumulation, (3) referencing intraday high extrema from a prior setup stage, or (4) detecting multi-bar sequential patterns. These require custom state management beyond the 3-field condition structure.
translation_notes: This is a complex multi-stage DeMark Sequential pattern requiring: (1) initial setup detection, (2) intersection validation, (3) stateful countdown tracking across non-consecutive bars where close < close[2-bars-ago], (4) disqualification rules monitoring sell setup patterns, (5) reference to the highest intraday high from the setup stage. A single condition with prev_close cannot capture this logic. Recommend implementing as a custom indicator or state machine outside the standard rule engine.
